title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After dinner, Patrick and Christine Redfern walk to a location near "Sunshine Cliff," a series of stone recesses carved into a cliffside. Christine confronts Patrick, accusing him of choosing this specific location only because Mrs. Marshall was coming there. She claims Patrick has fallen in love with Elena Marshall and warns that Elena is a bad person who will hurt him. Patrick dismisses her concerns as jealousy and paranoia, insisting he was merely making small talk. He urges Christine to be reasonable, and they return to the hotel. Hercule Poirot, having eavesdropped on the argument from a nearby corner, tells Hastings that the signs of a coming murder were already present.

Elsewhere, on the eastern side of the island, Rosamund Darnley and Kenneth Marshall sit on the grass overlooking a bay. Rosamund reminisces about their past before abruptly suggesting that Kenneth should divorce his wife, Elena. She cites Elena's bad reputation and the fact that she does not get along well with Kenneth's daughter, Linda. Kenneth becomes angry at the suggestion, expressing his disdain for the ease with which people enter and exit marriages. He asserts that marriage is a responsibility and that he intends to honor his commitment to Elena "till death do us part." Later, while driving a narrow road back to Redfern Bay, Horace Blatt—a tall, balding man with a red face who enjoys being the center of attention—nearly hits Christine Redfern, forcing her to press herself against a mountain wall to avoid the car.
